title: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, Upsert, and Mutation Evidence · Query Trace: Mutation Returning Audit
summary: A ticket import and reassignment workflow must change only intended rows and retain evidence of what happened. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql mutation returning audit increment in a guarded ticket-mutation workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Capture affected identity, before/after evidence, actor, reason, and changed count for mutations.
Use a named uniqueness conflict and explicit update policy for idempotent ingestion.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

steps.title: Predict Mutation Returning Audit · Theory Mutation Returning Audit
steps.instruction:
theory case — Import ticket 204 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Mutation Returning Audit contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Mutation Returning Audit · Theory Mutation Returning Audit.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture affected identity, before/after evidence, actor, reason, and changed count for mutations. For Predict Mutation Returning Audit · Theory Mutation Returning Audit, keep Mutation Returning Audit connected to executable relational evidence.
